commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=65760e4890c07e0152f67f4c0b7230f59af5f784)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes CLI-backed isolated cron runs to use non-owner loopback authorization, adds a scoped MCP bearer-token allowlist for cron self-removal, extends CLI/gateway tests and types, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: run an isolated cron job through a compatible CLI runtime with `toolsAllow: ["cron"]`; on the PR branch the scoped token only carries an owner-only allowlist, while loopback tool construction has no job id/self-remove scope.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair can thread the active cron job scope through the existing loopback bearer/request/tool-resolution path and add regression coverage.
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger
